risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain substantial, creating uncertainty for project implementation acceleration and institutional reform efforts in Guinea's mining and environmental sectors.

risk_title: Delays in Institutional Capacity and Reform Implementation
risk_summary: Institutional capacity constraints are limiting the pace of key reforms needed for the project, with only 25% disbursement rate and multiple regulatory frameworks still in draft stages, suggesting governance fragility in implementing agencies.
evidence_quote: The Project has to confirm implementation acceleration (currently 25% disbursement rate) supported by technical assistance recruited to support the ongoing reforms to address key challenges including the development of the Simandou Iron ore mine.

risk_title: Stakeholder Engagement and Transparency Challenges in Mining Sector
risk_summary: Weak institutionalization of citizen consultation mechanisms and stakeholder engagement in mining governance indicates tensions between government development priorities and community participation requirements.
evidence_quote: Channels institutionalized for citizens to provide input on planning and execution of infrastructure projects (Yes/No) [Status] No [Current Date] 08-Oct-2024

risk_title: Governance Fragility in Environmental and Social Monitoring
risk_summary: Weak institutional capacity for environmental and social monitoring of mining operations, with limited citizen involvement in oversight committees, reflects governance gaps that could undermine project objectives.
evidence_quote: Annual environmental monitoring inspections of large infrastructure projects undertaken with citizen consultation (Number) [Actual Current] 37.00 [Date] 31-Dec-2023 [Target] 12.00 Dec/2026
